headline: Disability rights registration covers only 600,000 of 4.8 million
context: The Social Welfare Department's OKU registry recorded only 600,000 persons with disabilities in 2025, against an estimated 4.8 million Malaysians with disabilities.

cards.big: Only 600,000 of an estimated 4.8 million disabled Malaysians are registered
cards.sub: Without OKU card, persons with disabilities cannot access government support

cards.big: Persons with Disabilities Act 2008 lacks enforcement penalties for non-compliance
cards.sub: Act provides rights framework but no mechanism to compel accessibility

cards.big: Malaysia ratified CRPD in 2010 but has not signed the Optional Protocol
cards.sub: Individual complaints mechanism unavailable to Malaysian PWDs

cards.big: PWD employment rate stands at 0.2% in public sector versus 1% target
cards.sub: Stigma and inaccessible workplaces cited as primary barriers by 82% of respondents

cards.big: 4.2 million unregistered means the system excludes those it claims to serve
JKM allocated RM42 million for disability welfare programs in Budget 2025
